one_line: You may also qualify — low income often means free or low-cost health coverage for you and your family.
why_short: Medi-Cal covers doctor visits, prescriptions, dental, and mental health at little or no cost if your household income is below the limit.

citation.note: California Department of Health Care Services — Medi-Cal eligibility overview

next_step: Apply online at coveredca.com or call 1-800-300-1506 (Covered California)

one_line: You may also qualify — pregnant, postpartum, or raising a child under 5 and income-eligible means free healthy food benefits.
why_short: WIC provides vouchers for nutritious foods, breastfeeding support, and health referrals for young families with low to moderate incomes.

citation.note: California Department of Public Health — WIC program overview

next_step: Find your local WIC office at cdph.ca.gov/wic or call 1-888-942-9675

one_line: You may also qualify — filing taxes with low earned income can unlock a refundable California credit paid directly to you.
why_short: CalEITC and the Young Child Tax Credit are refundable state tax credits for working Californians with low income, including those with an ITIN.

citation.note: California Franchise Tax Board — CalEITC and YCTC eligibility

next_step: File your California state taxes at ftb.ca.gov or get free filing help at MyFreeTaxes.com

one_line: You may also qualify — income-based discounts can cut your electric and gas bill significantly every month.
why_short: CARE gives a monthly discount on electric and gas service; FERA offers an additional reduction for households of three or more that narrowly miss CARE income limits.

citation.note: California Public Utilities Commission — CARE and FERA program details

next_step: Apply through your utility provider or visit cpuc.ca.gov/care

one_line: You may also qualify — qualifying for any public assistance program can get you discounted phone or internet service.
why_short: California LifeLine reduces monthly costs for home phone and wireless service for income-eligible residents or those already enrolled in programs like Medi-Cal or CalFresh.

citation.note: California LifeLine Program — eligibility and enrollment

next_step: Check eligibility and apply at californialifeline.com or call 1-866-272-0349

one_line: You may also qualify — school-age children in income-eligible households can eat breakfast and lunch at school at no cost.
why_short: The National School Lunch and Breakfast Programs provide free or reduced-price meals at school each day for children whose household income meets federal guidelines.

citation.note: California Department of Education — school meal program information

next_step: Contact your child's school office or visit cde.ca.gov to find the application

one_line: You may also qualify — families with school-age kids may automatically receive summer grocery benefits when school is out.
why_short: SUN Bucks provides an EBT card loaded with grocery benefits each summer for children who qualify for free or reduced-price school meals, with no separate application required for most families.

citation.note: California Department of Social Services — SUN Bucks Summer EBT overview

next_step: Visit cdss.ca.gov/sun-bucks or call 1-877-847-3663 to check your child's status

program: Rx Cost Pathways (Rxassist / Covered CA / Medi-Cal Rx)
one_line: You may also qualify — several programs can lower or eliminate out-of-pocket prescription costs based on your income and coverage.
why_short: California residents can access free or deeply discounted prescriptions through Medi-Cal's pharmacy benefit, manufacturer patient assistance programs, and nonprofit Rx discount networks.

citation.note: RxAssist — patient assistance program directory; DHCS Medi-Cal pharmacy benefit

next_step: Search manufacturer assistance programs at rxassist.org or talk to your pharmacist about GoodRx or Medi-Cal Rx

one_line: You may also qualify — rental assistance vouchers can help cover the gap between what you can afford and market rent.
why_short: The Housing Choice Voucher program helps low-income renters afford stable housing by paying a portion of rent directly to landlords; waitlists are managed by local housing authorities.

citation.note: California Department of Housing and Community Development — Housing Choice Vouchers

next_step: Find your local housing authority waitlist at hud.gov/program_offices/public_indian_housing/pha/contacts or call 1-800-955-2232
